case_title: LINDSTROM & SON BUILDERS VS. CANDLESTICK VIEW HOMEOWNERS'

docket_entries.description: OPPOSITION TO RESP.'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD; REQ. FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT FILED BY PETITIONER LINDSTROM & SON BUILDERS

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT CANDLESTICK VIEW HOMEOWNERS' PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION IS DENIED. (CCP 1286.2(A)(3) & (4).) PETITIONER'S OBJECTIONS TO BAUGH DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED AS TO OBJECTION NOS. 1 - 6 AND OVERRULED OBJECTION NOS. 7 - 9, 10. PETITIONER'S OBJECTIONS TO BIZIEFF DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. RESPONDENT HAS NOT PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO DEMONSTRATE THAT ITS RIGHTS WERE SUBSTANTIALLY PREJUDICED BY MISCONDUCT OF A NEUTRAL ARBITRATOR OR THAT THE ARBITRATOR EXCEEDED HIS POWERS AND THE AWARD CANNOT BE CORRECTED WITHOUT AFFECTING THE MERITS OF THE DECISION UPON THE CONTROVERSY SUBMITTED. (CCP 1286.2(A)(3) & (4).) THE DECLARATION OF ANDREW M. BAUGH DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT THE ARBITRATOR AFFIRMATIVELY ORDERED PRODUCTION OF THE RAA REPORTS DURING THE ARBITRATION. THE DECLARATION OF GORDON M. BIZIEFF IS INSUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE ARBITRATION AWARD VIOLATES A PUBLIC POLICY. THE INTRODUCTION OF THE DECLARATION OF DANIEL L ROTTINGHAUR IN RESPONSE TO OBJECTIONS IS IMPROPER AND NOT CONSIDERED BY THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment (Ccp 1286, 1287.4)
Off Calendar Mar-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Peter J. Linn, Esq. of Bishop Barry Drath, Tel. 510.596.0888, for petitioner and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PETITIONER LINDSTROM & SON BUILDERS' Motion For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment (CCP 1286, 1287.4) is granted. No opposition filed. Order and Judgment signed. Judge: Peter Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR# 12353.

PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Jan-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: PETER LINN, ESQ., 510.596.0888, FOR PETITIONER LINDSTROM & SON BUILDERS.
DANIEL LACOUNT, ESQ., AND ANDY BAUGH, ESQ., 925.838.2090, FOR RESPONDENT CANDLESTICK VIEW HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: RESPONDENT CANDLESTICK VIEW HOMEOWNERS' PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION is Denied. (CCP 1286.2(a)(3) & (4).) Petitioner's objections to Baugh Declaration are sustained as to objection nos. 1 - 6 and overruled objection nos. 7 - 9, 10. Petitioner's objections to Bizieff Declaration are sustained. Respondent has not presented sufficient evidence to demonstrate that its rights were substantially prejudiced by misconduct of a neutral arbitrator or that the arbitrator exceeded his powers and the award cannot be corrected without affecting the merits of the decision upon the controversy submitted. (CCP 1286.2(a)(3) & (4).) The declaration of Andrew M. Baugh does not demonstrate that the Arbitrator affirmatively ordered production of the RAA Reports during the arbitration. The declaration of Gordon M. Bizieff is insufficient to demonstrate that the arbitration award violates a public policy. The introduction of the declaration of Daniel L Rottinghaur in response to objections is improper and not considered by the Court. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
